name: The Carter Center
mission: It seeks to prevent and resolve conflicts, enhance freedom and democracy, and improve health.
theoryOfChange: The Carter Center has a published "Peace Programs Strategic Plan" (2022-2027) which outlines its vision, goals, and objectives for preventing or mitigating the impact of war and conflict and promoting democracy, human rights, and the rule of law around the world. This strategic plan describes the world The Carter Center envisions, with countries and communities that are more peaceful, inclusive, participatory, empowered, equitable, transparent, and accountable. The plan also details new programming tools and approaches, including enhanced collaboration with local stakeholders, extensive integration and coordination across program areas for more impactful programs, and an analytical approach incorporating cross-cutting themes like gender, climate change, and health. It commits to implementing high-quality, evidence-based programs and includes a plan for creating an implementation plan to ensure that the vision, goals, and objectives can be measured and captured.
scoreSummary: The Carter Center Inc shows good impact and moderate alignment (major risk deductions applied).

evaluationSources: New York University Caregiver Intervention (NYUCI) demonstration projects

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Context dependent profile. Consider cause fit, risk profile, and evidence maturity before deciding.

dimension_explanations.alignment: The organization offers a medium fit for donors interested in global advocacy and human rights, providing pathways for sadaqah contributions [3]. Its focus on high-level policy and international standards represents a long-term advocacy approach rather than immediate emergency relief [2].
dimension_explanations.impact: The Center demonstrates direct service impact through its role in international election monitoring and conflict mediation [2]. While it maintains a robust program expense ratio of 81.4%, specific cost-per-beneficiary metrics remain unavailable, leading to a medium impact score [1][4].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Carter Center Inc works to advance global peace and health by resolving conflicts and safeguarding democratic processes through international election observation.
strengths: High program expense ratio of 81.4%, ensuring the majority of funds reach core missions
Exceptional fundraising efficiency with a cost of only $0.07 per $1 raised
Strong leadership in establishing international normative standards for credible elections
baseline_narrative.summary: Founded on the mission to prevent conflict and enhance freedom, The Carter Center operates major initiatives including its Conflict Resolution Program and Election Observation efforts [2][4]. The organization reported a total revenue of $162,384,287, maintaining a high level of operational liquidity with 91.0 months of working capital [1][4].

case_against.mitigation_notes: The Center's large endowment is intended to ensure the permanent continuation of its peace and health programs regardless of political climates.
risk_factors: <cite id="16">Over 8 years of operating reserves held in net assets</cite>
<cite id="22">81.4% of expenses are classified as domestic burn</cite> despite international mission focus
case_against.summary: The primary concern is the <cite id="16">extremely high level of liquid assets</cite>, which may suggest that additional donations are not immediately urgent.

confidence.notes: Strong financial and programmatic data available, though third-party ratings are incomplete.

alignment.explanation: The charity is <cite id="20">SADAQAH-ELIGIBLE</cite> and serves many Muslim-majority regions like <cite id="7">Palestine and Sudan</cite>, though it lacks a faith-based framework.
alignment.improvement: The organization could improve alignment by <cite id="21">clarifying the sadaqah-applicability</cite> of its humanitarian health programs for Muslim donors.

credibility.explanation: The Center is highly credible due to its <cite id="14">large, independent board</cite> and <cite id="1">42-year operating history</cite>. It maintains <cite id="18">95% independent board membership</cite> and rigorous financial audits.
credibility.improvement: To improve transparency, the organization should work toward <cite id="19">meeting all 20 BBB Wise Giving Alliance standards</cite>, as current data shows no active review completion.

impact.explanation: Impact is driven by <cite id="9">direct service delivery</cite> and a <cite id="5">81.4% program expense ratio</cite>. The Center has successfully <cite id="12">reduced Guinea worm cases</cite> to near-zero levels.
impact.improvement: The Center should <cite id="17">publish a time-bound plan</cite> to deploy its excess unrestricted reserves into active mission delivery.

donor_fit_matrix.evidence_rigor: Grade B - Verified outcomes and documented Theory of Change

grantmaking_profile.grant_strategy: Funds specialized partners to execute health and democracy initiatives globally.

rich_narrative.headline: The Carter Center Inc works to advance global peace and health by resolving conflicts and safeguarding democratic processes through international election observation.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who value long-term institutional stability and want to support high-level diplomatic and public health interventions with a secular, non-partisan approach.
donor_motivations: Value the legacy of President Jimmy Carter's humanitarian work
Prioritize the eradication of specific diseases like Guinea worm
Believe in the importance of international election monitoring for democracy
Prefer donating to organizations with massive financial security
giving_considerations: The organization is extremely well-funded with over $1 billion in assets
Donations support broad institutional goals rather than individual small-scale projects
Focus is on high-level policy and systemic health interventions
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking to fulfill Zakat obligations or those who prefer funding smaller, under-capitalized grassroots organizations.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: The Center provides verified outcome data for its health programs and has established global standards for its peace work, though cost-per-beneficiary data is missing.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: The Center uses a combination of high-level advocacy, international monitoring, and direct community-based health interventions to break cycles of conflict and disease.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Verified outcomes like the reduction in Guinea worm cases prove the technical efficacy of the Center's field operations.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: While the Center has massive reserves, its ambitious goal of global disease eradication requires sustained annual cash flow.

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by its <cite id="1">presidential founding</cite> and unique dual focus on <cite id="2">election integrity</cite> and disease eradication.

similar_organizations.differentiator: More focused on political party building
similar_organizations.name: National Democratic Institute

similar_organizations.differentiator: Primarily a funder rather than an implementer
similar_organizations.name: Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation

strengths.detail: The Center ensures that <cite id="5">81.4% of its budget</cite> goes directly to its peace and health missions, exceeding common sector benchmarks.
The organization demonstrates high efficiency by spending only <cite id="10">$0.08 to raise every $1</cite> in charitable contributions.
The Center has developed <cite id="6">33 detailed commitments</cite> that serve as international standards for credible and genuine elections.

strengths_deep_dive: The Carter Center’s health initiatives demonstrate remarkable technical success, particularly in the <cite id="11">Guinea Worm Eradication Program</cite>. By 2020, the program reduced human cases of the disease to <cite id="12">just 27 globally</cite>, moving closer to making it the second human disease in history to be eradicated. This success is supported by a <cite id="13">strong theory of change</cite> and a governance structure featuring a <cite id="14">43-member board</cite> that provides extensive oversight. Furthermore, the Center acts as a significant grantmaker, distributing <cite id="15">over $20 million in foreign grants</cite> to local partners to facilitate international health and peace operations.
rich_narrative.summary: founded in 1982 by former U.S. President Jimmy Carter and Rosalynn Carter, The Carter Center operates as a non-partisan institution dedicated to <cite id="1">preventing and resolving conflicts</cite> and improving global health. The organization manages a diverse portfolio of initiatives, ranging from <cite id="2">international election observation</cite> to the near-eradication of Guinea worm disease. In 2023, the Center reported <cite id="3">total revenue of $153,773,756</cite>, reflecting a <cite id="4">three-year revenue growth rate of 11.9%</cite>. The charity maintains a lean operational structure, directing <cite id="5">81.4% of its total expenses toward program services</cite>. While its peace programs focus on <cite id="6">establishing international normative standards for elections</cite>, its health programs target neglected tropical diseases in regions such as <cite id="7">Nigeria, Ethiopia, and South Sudan</cite>. The Center operates with significant financial security, holding <cite id="8">over $1.1 billion in net assets</cite>, which provides a unique level of stability for long-term humanitarian commitments. For donors, this represents an opportunity to support a highly professionalized organization with <cite id="9">direct service delivery</cite> and a proven track record of institutional impact.
